data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Shares Jump 5% After Q3 Blowout, AI Chip Demand Soars
data.description:
Shares of Nvidia (NVDA, Financial) rose about 5% on early Thursday trading following fiscal third-quarter results that topped Wall Street forecasts.

The semiconductor company reported revenue of $57 billion, surpassing analysts' $54.9 billion estimate. Adjusted earnings per share reached $1.30, above the $1.25 consensus. Net income jumped 65% to $31.9 billion from $19.3 billion a year ago.

CEO Jensen Huang said demand for the company's Blackwell GPUs is “off the charts.” Nvidia expects roughly $65 billion in revenue for the current quarter, above the $61.7 billion analysts had projected.

Data center sales continue to drive growth, climbing to $51.2 billion, a 66% year-over-year increase. Of this, $43 billion came from compute-focused GPUs, while networking products added $8.2 billion.

Gaming, professional visualization, and automotive/robotics units also posted gains. DGX Spark AI desktops and Blackwell Ultra chips contributed to growth.

Nvidia noted limited sales in China due to geopolitical and competitive constraints. CFO Colette Kress confirmed $12.5 billion in share repurchases and $243 million in dividends during the quarter.

data.title: Why Tesla Trillion Price Tag Looks More Like a Bet on Elon Than the Business
data.description:
Tesla's valuation remains the central issue for investors as the company navigates mixed business signals and high market expectations.

The electric-vehicle maker trades at multiples that put it well above legacy automakers. At roughly a $1.34 trillion market capitalization, Tesla's sales multiple of 14x sits many times higher than peers Ford (F, Financial) and General Motors (GM, Financial), which trade at fraction-of-sales levels. That gap has puzzled some analysts and investors.

Automotive revenue still drives the bulk of Tesla's business, but growth has slowed from earlier in the decade. Its energy unit shows stronger growth but remains small relative to the stock's price tag. The expectation that either segment will close the valuation gap has not materialized.

Speculation about robotaxis and full self-driving (FSD) often fuels optimistic price targets. Tesla has run pilots and showcased concepts such as a Cybercab, yet commercial robotaxi revenues remain negligible. Independent competitors, notably Waymo, already offer paid, driverless rides in limited markets, underscoring the challenges ahead.

People continue discussing the AI-related products of Tesla and Optimus robots. Optimus has yet to receive any money, and the FSD packages are not yet mainstream items, yet a small number of buyers choose a paid version. Those facts make it difficult to jump to the thought that new lines will help to bring in big money somewhere.

There are two things that investors must balance: what would happen to the company in case Tesla abandons its ambitious plans, and the danger of the sky-high expectations having already been priced in.

To the portfolio managers, Tesla is more of a bet on Elon and the hype than on the numbers.

data.title: Nvidia and Microsoft Strike Massive AI Deal With Anthropic -- What It Means For Investors
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT, Financial), Nvidia (NVDA, Financial) and Anthropic outlined a major partnership Tuesday that includes strategic investments and a long-term compute pact.

Nvidia and Microsoft plan to invest up to $10 billion and up to $5 billion, respectively, while Anthropic agreed to commit roughly $30 billion of spending on Microsoft Azure and to contract additional compute capacity up to one gigawatt.

The investments value Anthropic at about $350 billion, according to media reports, up from a $183 billion valuation after a September $13 billion raise.

Anthropic's frontier Claude models, Claude Sonnet 4.5, Claude Opus 4.1 and Claude Haiku 4.5 will be available on Microsoft's Azure AI Foundry, making Claude accessible across the top three cloud platforms.

Nvidia and Anthropic will also work to optimize those models for future Nvidia architectures and to use Anthropic's Grace Blackwell and Vera Rubin systems within the compute commitment.

For investors, the deal signals deepening collaboration in AI and cloud infrastructure, potentially boosting growth prospects for MSFT and NVDA, though the large commitments also carry execution and valuation risks.

data.title: Microsoft and Nvidia  to Invest in Anthropic as Startup Commits $30 Billion to Azure
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT, Financials) and Nvidia (NVDA, Financials) said they plan to invest in Anthropic under a new partnership that includes a $30 billion commitment by the AI developer to use Microsoft's Azure cloud services. A person familiar with the matter said both companies will participate in Anthropic's next funding round, though terms were not disclosed.

Nvidia plans to commit up to $10 billion and Microsoft up to $5 billion. The companies said the collaboration will expand compute capacity for Anthropic's Claude models and strengthen joint go-to-market efforts. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella said both firms expect to be customers of one another's technologies, adding that OpenAI remains a critical partner.

The agreement underscores the heavy computing demands of training frontier AI models and reflects efforts by major cloud and semiconductor providers to diversify partnerships beyond OpenAI. Analysts said the deal reduces dependence on a single model developer as competition among AI companies intensifies.

Anthropic has been expanding quickly since its launch in 2021, driven by strong enterprise adoption of the Claude platform. The company was recently valued at $183 billion and is forecasting annualized revenue growth that could approach $26 billion next year. It now serves more than 300,000 business customers.

Under the new arrangement, Anthropic will work with Nvidia to develop chips and model-optimization tools and will reserve up to 1 gigawatt of compute using Nvidia's Grace Blackwell and Vera Rubin hardware. Industry estimates put the cost of 1 gigawatt of AI computing at $20 billion to $25 billion.

data.title: Hanmi to Acquire Remaining Shares of Aptose Biosciences (APTOF)
data.description:
Key Takeaways:

  • Aptose Biosciences (APTOF, Financial) is set to be fully acquired by Hanmi Pharmaceutical's subsidiary, HS North America Ltd., at a 28% premium over the 30-day average price.
  • The acquisition is backed by over US$30 million in debt facilities aimed at supporting Aptose's drug development initiatives.
  • The deal is pending shareholder and regulatory approvals, with a Special Meeting scheduled before January 16, 2026.

Aptose Biosciences (APTOF) has announced an agreement with Hanmi Pharmaceutical's subsidiary, HS North America Ltd., to acquire all remaining shares of Aptose. Shareholders will receive C$2.41 per share, a 28% premium over the 30-day average price of C$1.88. Hanmi, already holding 19.93% of Aptose, will complete the acquisition, supported by over US$30 million in debt facilities for Aptose's drug development. The deal awaits shareholder and regulatory approvals, with a Special Meeting set before January 16, 2026.

Aptose Biosciences Inc is a clinical-stage biotechnology company committed to discovering and developing personalized therapies addressing unmet medical needs in oncology. The company's small molecule cancer therapeutics pipeline includes products designed to provide single agent efficacy and to enhance the efficacy of other anti-cancer therapies and regimens without overlapping toxicities. Aptose operates within the healthcare sector, specifically in the biotechnology industry, and is currently trading on the OTCPK exchange. The company has a market capitalization of approximately C$4.07 million.

Financial Health Analysis

Despite its promising pipeline, Aptose Biosciences faces significant financial challenges. The company has reported zero revenue growth over the past three years, reflecting its status as a clinical-stage entity. The earnings per share (EPS) stands at a negative C$8.17, indicating ongoing operational losses. Furthermore, the company's current ratio and quick ratio are both at 0.36, suggesting liquidity constraints.

On the balance sheet front, Aptose's debt-to-equity ratio is negative at -0.79, which may indicate a reliance on debt financing. The return on assets (ROA) is significantly negative at -233.71%, and the return on invested capital (ROIC) is -1445.01%, highlighting inefficiencies in asset utilization and capital allocation.

Valuation & Market Sentiment

In terms of valuation, Aptose's enterprise value to EBITDA is -0.83, reflecting the company's negative earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization. The Relative Strength Index (RSI-14) is at 73.55, indicating that the stock may be overbought in the short term. Moving averages show a 20-day SMA of 1.37, a 50-day SMA of 1.26, and a 200-day SMA of 2.01, suggesting a downward trend over the longer term.

Institutional ownership is minimal at 0.94%, while insider ownership is substantial at 72.01%, indicating a high level of insider confidence in the company's prospects. However, the Piotroski F-Score is low, suggesting weak financial health.

Risk Assessment

Aptose Biosciences' financial health grades indicate potential risks. The Altman Z-Score is not provided, but the low F-Score and negative Sloan Ratio highlight concerns about the quality of earnings and operational efficiency. The company's volatility is high at 105.77, and its beta is 0.17, suggesting limited correlation with broader market movements.

Sector-specific risks include the inherent uncertainties in drug development and regulatory approvals. The upcoming shareholder meeting and regulatory reviews are critical catalysts that could impact the company's future trajectory.

data.title: NVDA: Billionare Ken Griffin Loads Up on Nvidia Ahead of Key Earnings
data.description:
Billionaire Ken Griffin tweaked Citadel's chip bets in Q3, loading up on Nvidia (NVDA, Financial) while trimming Intel (INTC, Financial), a move that highlights growing conviction in AI hardware leaders. Citadel bought about 1.73 million NVDA shares, boosting its stake to roughly 9.82 million shares worth about $1.82 billion.

At the same time, Citadel sold roughly 10.02 million INTC shares, cutting its Intel position as sentiment around Intel's turnaround remains mixed.

Analysts have reacted by leaning bullish on Nvidia: HSBC's Frank Lee upgraded NVDA to Buy and set a street-high $320 target, citing an expanding AI GPU addressable market.

Nvidia has a lot of confidence in the broad market. Reports have been mainly good with a large number of analysts giving a recommendation to buy Nvidia as well as a high price target due to heavy demand for data-centre GPUs.

Nonetheless, the valuation and the amount of money that large cloud companies are spending remain of concern to some investors.

They will monitor whether Citadel's investment in Nvidia is an indication of additional large-scale investors investing in AI-centered chip vendors or a simple change of direction in Citadel as a portion of its portfolio.

data.title: Apple Reclaims China Market -- iPhone 17 Drives Share to 25%
data.description:
Apple (AAPL, Financial) found fresh momentum in China as iPhone demand jumped, pushing the company to about 25% of the country's smartphone market, according to a Bloomberg report citing Counterpoint Research. Successor models, from the 5,999-yuan iPhone 17 to the 8,999-yuan 17 Pro Max, sold noticeably better than their iPhone 16 predecessors, helping Apple regain share lost earlier in 2025.

Counterpoint analyst Ivan Lam says new-model sales now account for more than 80% of Apple's unit shipments, and rising average selling prices should boost top-line growth. Lam warns competition remains possible, Huawei plans to launch the Mate 80 series on Nov. 25, but he sees strong momentum behind Apple's lineup and no clear signs of a sharp slowdown.

The rebound demonstrates that Apple is not in decline of its products, and those who have more to spend continue to purchase. This is a blessing to the services and accessories provided by Apple in China. In case the prices remain high, they may increase revenue significantly even in the face of stiff competition. Investors will continue to monitor China closely, where the phone cycle continues and the local competitors introduce new models, which may influence the number of shares in the short term.

data.title:
Apple Jumps to 25% Market Share in China
        (Status: Pending)
data.description:
Apple (AAPL, Financial) is enjoying a real comeback moment in China. A new Bloomberg report says iPhone sales have picked up so sharply that Apple now controls 25% of the Chinese smartphone market something that didn't seem likely earlier this year.

Counterpoint Research, which tracked the data, told Bloomberg that the entire iPhone 17 lineup is outperforming last year's models by a wide margin. Whether it's the 5,999-yuan (about $850) iPhone 17 or the top-end 8,999-yuan 17 Pro Max, each one is selling better than its iPhone 16 counterpart by double-digit percentages. Analyst Ivan Lam added that more than 80% of Apple's unit sales in China are now coming from the new models, and the higher price points are naturally helping Apple's revenue climb.

Lam did say Huawei's Mate 80 launch on November 25 could shake things up, but right now Apple's run looks strong. “There's a lot of momentum behind Apple,” he noted, adding there's no obvious sign that demand is about to fall off.

It's a big turnaround from early 2025, when Apple was losing share to Chinese rivals and dealing with weaker shipments. Investors will watch whether this iPhone 17 streak holds into the crucial holiday stretch.
